title: Geography: Countries & Capitals
description: How well do you know the capitals and geography of countries around the world?
subject: Geography

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital of Australia. It was purpose-built as the capital in 1913 as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.prompt: Which country has the largest land area in the world?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country by land area, covering about 17.1 million km² — nearly twice the size of Canada.

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Brazil?
questions.explanation: Brasília has been Brazil's capital since 1960, when it replaced Rio de Janeiro. It was planned and built from scratch.

questions.prompt: On which continent is the Sahara Desert located?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert is in North Africa, spanning over 9 million km² across multiple countries.

questions.prompt: The Amazon River flows primarily through which country?
questions.explanation: About 60% of the Amazon Basin lies within Brazil. The river flows roughly 6,400 km before emptying into the Atlantic Ocean.
